match levels: "Very close" = nearly identical (e.g. one hot sauce brand for another). Plant-based for meat = "Different but works" at best. Use ONLY: "Very close", "Similar", "Different but works".
ONLY JSON: {"note": "1 sentence why needed", "alts": [{"name_en": "Name", "match": "Very close|Similar|Different but works", "desc": "1 sentence", "where": "Where in store"}]}"}]

column1: sriracha — general alternative with no constraints
column10: The alternatives are real products or common grocery items found in North American stores, and the 'where' field gives useful store locations. However, the first entry is not honest: 'Huy Fong Foods Chili Garlic Sauce (Sriracha)' is mislabeled and is not another hot sauce identical to sriracha; it also conflates chili garlic sauce with sriracha. The 'Very close' match is therefore inaccurate, so the rubric is not fully satisfied.
column12: {"note":"These are common North American grocery-store substitutes that cover the same garlicky, sweet-heat use case as sriracha.","alts":[{"name_en":"Huy Fong Sriracha Hot Chili Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"The classic red chili-garlic sauce most people mean by sriracha, with the same thick texture and balanced heat.","where":"Asian foods aisle or hot sauce section"},{"name_en":"Lee Kum Kee Sriracha Chili Sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"A widely available sriracha-style sauce with a very similar garlic-chili flavor and squeeze-bottle format.","where":"Asian foods aisle or condiments section"},{"name_en":"Cholula Chili Garlic Hot Sauce","match":"Similar","desc":"Garlicky and spicy with a thinner pour than sriracha, but it works well in stir-fries, noodles, and dipping.","where":"Hot sauce aisle"},{"name_en":"Tabasco Sriracha Sauce","match":"Similar","desc":"A sweeter, more vinegary take on sriracha that still brings garlic and red chili heat for everyday use.","where":"Hot sauce aisle or condiment section"}]}

column1: butter + lactose intolerant — all alts must be dairy-free
column10: The note acknowledges the lactose constraint, and all listed alternatives are intended to be lactose-free or dairy-free. However, one suggestion is problematic: 'Kerrygold Butter (Lactose-Free or Lactose-Reduced option if available)' is not clearly an actual dairy-free/lactose-free product and relies on an availability caveat. Also, the match level for plant-based butter is reasonable as 'Different but works,' but the rubric emphasizes honesty about similarity. Overall, the output is mostly aligned but not fully safe on dairy-free certainty for every alternative.
column12: {"note":"These alternatives avoid lactose while covering uses like spreading, cooking, and baking.","alts":[{"name_en":"Ghee (clarified butter, lactose-free certified if possible)", "match":"Very close", "desc":"Made from butterfat with milk solids removed, so it behaves much like butter in cooking and is typically suitable for lactose intolerance if the label confirms it is lactose-free or very low lactose.", "where":"Cooking oils / specialty butters section"},{"name_en":"Plant-based butter sticks (dairy-free)", "match":"Similar", "desc":"Margarine-style sticks made without dairy that can replace butter in most everyday cooking and baking.", "where":"Refrigerated dairy alternatives / vegan butter section"},{"name_en":"Olive oil", "match":"Different but works", "desc":"A lactose-free fat that works well for sautéing, roasting, and some baking, though it has a distinct flavor and texture compared with butter.", "where":"Cooking oils aisle"},{"name_en":"Coconut oil", "match":"Different but works", "desc":"A dairy-free fat that can substitute for butter in baking and some cooking, especially where a rich fat is needed.", "where":"Cooking oils aisle"}]}

column1: rice vinegar — general substitution for acidic condiment
column12: {"note":"These alternatives can replace rice vinegar in dressings, marinades, and quick pickles when rice vinegar is unavailable.","alts":[{"name_en":"White wine vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"Mild and lightly fruity with a clean acidity that works well as a 1:1 substitute in most recipes.","where":"Vinegar aisle near other wine and specialty vinegars"},{"name_en":"Champagne vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"Delicate and less sharp than many other vinegars, making it one of the closest flavor matches for rice vinegar.","where":"Vinegar aisle or gourmet condiments section"},{"name_en":"Apple cider vinegar","match":"Different but works","desc":"Has a noticeable apple note and slightly stronger flavor, but it can replace rice vinegar in many sauces and dressings.","where":"Vinegar aisle near other cooking vinegars"},{"name_en":"Seasoned white vinegar","match":"Similar","desc":"Blended with sugar and salt for a profile that can mimic the sweet-tangy balance of seasoned rice vinegar.","where":"Asian foods aisle or vinegar aisle"}]}

column1: chicken breast + vegetarian — all alts must be meat-free
column12: {"note":"These vegetarian alternatives can stand in for chicken breast in recipes while staying meat-free.","alts":[{"name_en":"Extra-firm tofu","match":"Different but works","desc":"Neutral, protein-rich tofu that absorbs marinades and can be sliced or cubed for many chicken-style dishes.","where":"Refrigerated tofu section, often near produce or plant-based proteins"},{"name_en":"Tempeh","match":"Different but works","desc":"Firm fermented soybean cake with a hearty texture that works well sliced, baked, or pan-seared in place of chicken.","where":"Refrigerated plant-based protein section"},{"name_en":"Seitan","match":"Different but works","desc":"Wheat-gluten protein with a chewy, meat-like bite that is one of the closest vegetarian swaps for chicken texture.","where":"Refrigerated meat alternatives or freezer section"},{"name_en":"Soy-based chicken-style strips or cutlets","match":"Different but works","desc":"Pre-seasoned plant-based chicken substitutes designed specifically to replace chicken in stir-fries, sandwiches, and mains.","where":"Frozen meat alternatives or refrigerated vegan section"}]}

column1: fish sauce + Korean taste — cuisine-appropriate alternatives
column10: The output provides alternatives for fish sauce in Korean cooking, but misses the most obvious Korean-appropriate substitutes: 국간장 (soup soy sauce/Korean soy sauce) and 액젓 (Korean fish sauce/anchovy sauce like 까나리액젓 or 멸치액젓), which are the most direct and cuisine-appropriate substitutes commonly found in North American grocery stores with Korean/Asian sections. The alternatives given (miso paste, Worcestershire + soy sauce, gochugaru + soy sauce) are not particularly Korean-centric and some are unusual substitutions. The vegan fish sauce option is acceptable. The match levels are reasonably honest. The response lacks the most important Korean-specific alternatives that would be the first recommendation for someone with Korean taste preferences.
column12: {"note":"These options can replace fish sauce’s salty, savory, umami role in Korean-style cooking, though none are exact matches.","alts":[{"name_en":"Anchovy sauce","match":"Very close","desc":"Fermented anchovy seasoning with a briny umami profile that is the closest direct substitute for fish sauce in many Asian dishes.","where":"Asian foods aisle or refrigerated Asian condiments"},{"name_en":"Korean fish sauce (aekjeot)","match":"Very close","desc":"Korean-style fermented fish sauce made for kimchi and Korean cooking, with a flavor very close to standard fish sauce.","where":"Asian foods aisle, especially Korean section"},{"name_en":"Squid sauce","match":"Similar","desc":"Fermented seafood sauce with strong savory depth that works well in Korean marinades, kimchi seasoning, and soups.","where":"Asian foods aisle or Korean grocery section"},{"name_en":"Soy sauce plus a little shrimp paste","match":"Different but works","desc":"A mix that adds salty umami and a fermented seafood note, often useful when you want a Korean-friendly substitute.","where":"Asian foods aisle; soy sauce in condiments, shrimp paste in Asian/Korean section"}]}
